defence_context.notes: War enters 4th week. No ceasefire — Iran FM: 'We don't ask for ceasefire but war must end.' Trump considering 'winding down' but more Marines deploying. Cumulative: 1,444+ dead in Iran, 18+ in Israel, 21+ in Gulf states. Brent crude at $112-115/barrel.

notes: Day 22. War enters 4th week. IRAN FIRED 2 INTERMEDIATE-RANGE BALLISTIC MISSILES AT DIEGO GARCIA (~4,000km range) — both missed (1 failed mid-flight, 1 possibly intercepted by SM-3). First IRBM use, revealing greater range than admitted. US-Israel attacked Natanz nuclear enrichment facility (2nd time) — IAEA confirmed no radiation leak. Cumulative: Hengaw 6th report — 5,900 killed in Iran (5,305 military + 595 civilian), 18,000+ injured. 17 Israeli civilians killed, 3,834 injured. 13 US service members KIA + 6 in KC-135 crash. 886+ killed in Lebanon (111 children), 1M displaced. Oil $112-115/bbl. Iranian attacks down 90% from early war (Pentagon). 70%+ of Iran's missile launchers neutralized. Ukrainian drone tech deployed to Gulf for air defense.

defence_context.notes: Iranian drones struck Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery (730,000 bpd capacity) — Kuwait's largest. Fires at several operational units. Hit during Eid al-Fitr celebrations. No injuries reported. Refinery had been hit day before as well.

notes: Day 21. Iranian drones struck Kuwait's Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery (730,000 bpd), the country's largest, sparking fires at several units. Attack came during Eid al-Fitr. Part of Iran's broadening campaign against Gulf energy infrastructure after Israel struck South Pars gas field. Pentagon: Iran drone/missile attacks down 90% from early war days. Trump considering asking Congress for $200B to fund the war.

defence_context.notes: Continued attacks on Saudi military and energy infrastructure. Iran launched retaliatory strikes after Larijani killing. Part of broader energy escalation.

notes: Day 21. Continued Iranian strikes on Saudi Arabia targeting Prince Sultan Air Base and energy infrastructure. Part of energy-for-energy escalation cycle after Israel struck South Pars. Trump said considering 'winding down' military ops same day.

defence_context.notes: Day 21. Pentagon: Iranian attacks down 90% from early war. Netanyahu agreed to halt strikes on Iranian energy sites after Trump objected to South Pars strike.

notes: Day 21. Pentagon: Iranian drone/missile attacks down 90% from early war days. Netanyahu agreed to halt strikes on Iranian energy sites after Trump reacted angrily to South Pars attack. US lifted sanctions on 140M barrels of Iranian oil.

defence_context.notes: Day 21. Reduced intensity. Trump: considering 'winding down' military operations. But more Marines deploying to region.

notes: Day 21. Reduced intensity. Trump told reporters considering 'winding down' military efforts but 'I don't want to do a ceasefire.' More Marines deploying to region despite rhetoric. US reportedly drawing up plans for ground operation in Iran.

waves.notes: 5 missile salvos at Jerusalem and northern Israel within 1 hour.

defence_context.notes: 5 missile salvos at Jerusalem and northern Israel within 1 hour. Bazan oil refinery in Haifa damaged.

notes: Day 20. Iran launched 5 missile salvos at Jerusalem and northern Israel within one hour. Bazan oil refinery in Haifa damaged. Continued energy infrastructure targeting on both sides — Brent crude hit $115/barrel. 19 Iranian attack waves identified (exceptional day). US F-35 damaged by Iranian fire — emergency landing at US base. Brent crude briefly hit $119/bbl.

defence_context.notes: Day 20. UAE MFA called Iranian attacks 'Terrorist Attacks'. Iran warned 'zero restraint' if energy sites attacked again.

notes: Day 20. UAE Ministry of Foreign Affairs officially designated Iranian attacks as 'Terrorist Attacks'. Energy infrastructure strikes continued — Brent crude at $119/bbl briefly. Iran warned 'zero restraint' on energy targeting if provoked further.

defence_context.notes: Major strike on Ras Laffan gas facility caused extensive damage and sizeable fires at multiple LNG facilities. QatarEnergy CEO said attack cut ~17% of output potentially for 5 years. Qatar expelled Iranian military/security attaches within 24 hours.

notes: Day 19. MAJOR ESCALATION. Iran struck Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG facility causing 'extensive damage' and fires at multiple facilities. QatarEnergy CEO: ~17% of output cut, potentially for 5 years. Qatar declared Iranian embassy military attaches persona non grata — expelled within 24 hours. Retaliation for Israel's strike on Iran's South Pars gas field.

waves.notes: 13 waves total: 6 toward Tel Aviv (46.2%), 4 toward south (30.8%), 2 toward Jerusalem (15.4%), 1 toward north (7.7%).

defence_context.notes: 13 attack waves. Missile debris fell on Temple Mount (Al-Aqsa Mosque damage), Church of Holy Sepulchre, and Jewish Quarter. Synagogue shelter in Beit Shemesh struck — 9 civilians killed, dozens injured. 1 foreign worker killed near Hod Hasharon. 1 killed in Tel Aviv building strike.

notes: Day 19. Iran 'revenge' missile attack: 13 waves. Missile debris hit Temple Mount/Al-Aqsa, Church of Holy Sepulchre, Jewish Quarter in Jerusalem Old City. Synagogue shelter in Beit Shemesh struck — 9 civilians killed. Foreign worker killed in Moshav Adanim. Building struck in Tel Aviv killing 1. IRGC said attacks avenged Larijani assassination. Israel struck South Pars gas field (world's largest) with US coordination — triggering Iran's energy-for-energy retaliation cycle. Hengaw 5th report: 5,300 killed in Iran in 18 days (511 civilians, 4,789 military).

defence_context.notes: Continued attacks on UAE. Iran declared 'not a single liter of oil' would ship from the Gulf. UAE Foreign Ministry called attacks 'Terrorist Attacks' on Mar 19.

notes: Day 19. Continued drone and missile attacks on UAE. Iran threatened that 'not a single liter of oil' would ship from the Gulf. Part of broader energy infrastructure escalation after Israel struck South Pars gas field.

defence_context.notes: Retaliatory barrage after Israeli airstrikes killed senior officials Ali Larijani and Basij chief Gholamreza Soleimani. 2 Israeli civilians killed in Ramat Gan. Israel launched ground invasion of southern Lebanon same day.

notes: Day 18. Iran launched retaliatory missile barrage after Israel killed senior officials Ali Larijani and Basij commander Gholamreza Soleimani. 2 Israeli civilians killed in Ramat Gan. Israel launched ground invasion of southern Lebanon the same day. Israel also killed intelligence minister Esmaeil Khatib in overnight airstrike.

defence_context.notes: IDF struck ~400 Hezbollah targets: missile launchers, observation systems, weapons storage, HQs across Beirut, Beqaa, Sidon, and southern Lebanon. Ground invasion of southern Lebanon launched same day.

notes: Day 18. Israel launched ground invasion of southern Lebanon. IDF completed dozens of waves of strikes against Hezbollah infrastructure — about 400 targets struck including missile launchers, observation systems, weapons storage, and headquarters across Beirut, Beqaa, Sidon, and southern Lebanon.

defence_context.notes: Shahed-type drone struck fuel storage tank at Dubai International Airport, sparking large fire. Flights suspended 7+ hours. At least 1 killed in Abu Dhabi. Shah gas field also struck. As of Mar 17: 314 ballistic missiles, 15 cruise missiles, 1,672 UAVs fired at UAE total.

notes: Day 17. Shahed drone hit fuel tank outside Dubai International Airport — world's busiest airport suspended flights 7+ hours. Drone strike also sparked fire at Shah gas field. 1 killed in Abu Dhabi. Cumulative total as of Mar 17: 314 ballistic missiles, 15 cruise missiles, 1,672 UAVs fired at UAE. 8 killed, 157 injured total. CENTCOM: 100+ Iranian naval vessels destroyed including all 4 Soleimani-class corvettes. 3rd Iranian ballistic missile intercepted by NATO near Incirlik Air Base, Turkey.

defence_context.notes: Day 17. Saudi MoD downed multiple-drone barrage over Riyadh and oil-rich eastern region.

notes: Day 17. Saudi MoD downed multiple-drone barrage over Riyadh and oil-rich eastern region. Israel killed Larijani and Soleimani same day — triggering major escalation cycle.

defence_context.notes: Day 17. 3rd Iranian ballistic missile intercepted by NATO near Incirlik Air Base, Turkey. CENTCOM: 100+ Iranian naval vessels destroyed.

notes: Day 17. Missile attacks continued. 3rd Iranian ballistic missile intercepted by NATO near Incirlik Air Base, Turkey. CENTCOM: 100+ Iranian naval vessels destroyed, all 4 Soleimani-class ships sunk.

defence_context.notes: Day 16. Iran FM Araghchi: 'We never asked for a ceasefire.' EU rejected expanding naval operations around Hormuz despite Trump pressure.

notes: Day 16. Iran FM Araghchi: 'We never asked for a ceasefire.' EU rejected expanding naval operations around Hormuz despite Trump pressure. Trump criticized allies for not sending warships. Strikes continued on Tehran, Tabriz, Shiraz, Isfahan.

defence_context.notes: Day 16. UAE bore outsized share of Iranian attacks — more projectiles than any other country.

notes: Day 16. UAE continues to absorb outsized share of Iranian attacks — more missiles/drones than any other target country. Sustained daily attacks since Feb 28.

defence_context.notes: Drone struck Royal Tulip Al Rasheed Hotel hosting EU and Saudi Arabian delegation. Kata'ib Hezbollah commander Abu Ali al-Askari killed in separate airstrike.

notes: Day 15. Drone struck first two floors of Royal Tulip Al Rasheed Hotel in Baghdad Green Zone, hosting EU and Saudi delegation. Kata'ib Hezbollah security commander Abu Ali al-Askari killed in separate airstrike on Baghdad.

defence_context.notes: Day 15. US struck Kharg Island (90% of Iran's oil exports) — 90+ military targets. Trump: 'totally obliterated' all military targets on Kharg.

notes: Day 15. US struck Kharg Island — hub for 90% of Iran's oil exports — hitting 90+ military targets. Oil infrastructure spared. Trump: US 'totally obliterated' all military targets on Kharg. Iran threatened to reduce Gulf oil facilities to 'pile of ashes'. Iran death toll passed 1,400 (official); Hengaw estimates 4,900 killed (480 civilian).

waves.notes: 50 drones targeting Saudi Arabia within hours — largest single drone wave against the kingdom.

defence_context.notes: Saudi intercepted 2 ballistic missiles targeting Prince Sultan Air Base. 50 drones launched within hours — largest wave against Saudi Arabia in the war.

notes: Day 14. Saudi Arabia targeted with 50 drones within hours — largest single wave against the kingdom. Saudi MoD intercepted 2 ballistic missiles toward Prince Sultan Air Base at Al-Kharj. Shelter-in-place for Jeddah, Riyadh, Dhahran.

defence_context.notes: Day 14. Continued strikes. IDF estimated 3,000-4,000 Iranian soldiers/commanders killed.

notes: Day 14. Continued strikes on UAE. Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei issued first public statement. IDF estimated 3,000-4,000 Iranian military killed to date.

defence_context.notes: Day 14. IDF struck 200+ targets in Iran including missile launchers, air defense, weapons production, regime HQs.

notes: Day 14. Continued missile attacks on Israel. IDF struck 200+ targets in Iran — missile launchers, air defense, weapons production, regime HQs. Israel killed Basij paramilitary chief Gholamreza Soleimani (confirmed later).

defence_context.notes: Iranian drones hit Bahrain International Airport on Muharraq Island, setting fuel tanks on fire. Interior ministry confirmed airport targeted — material damage but no loss of life at airport.

notes: Night of Mar 11-12. Iranian drones struck Bahrain International Airport on Muharraq Island, setting fuel tanks on fire. Also struck fuel depot in Muharraq Governorate causing large fire. BDF has intercepted 112 ballistic missiles and 186 drones total since war began.

defence_context.notes: Iranian drones and missiles hit coalition military camps near Erbil and Baghdad, wounding US soldiers. Oil tanker Safesea Vishnu attacked by Iranian explosive boats near Basra — 1 Indian crew member killed.

notes: Day 13. Iranian strikes hit coalition bases near Erbil and Baghdad, wounding US soldiers. Oil tanker Safesea Vishnu attacked near Basra by Iranian explosive boats — 1 crew member killed. 6 US airmen killed when KC-135 tanker crashed in western Iraq during operations. Oil tanker Safesea Vishnu (Marshall Islands flag) attacked by Iranian explosive boats near Basra — 1 Indian crew member killed. 21 confirmed attacks on merchant ships by this date. 3 cargo ships struck off Iran's coast including one in Strait of Hormuz.

waves.notes: Combined rockets, missiles, drones from Lebanon (N) and Iran (E). At 12:35 UTC Israel struck Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon.

defence_context.notes: Combined attack from Lebanon and Iran simultaneously — coordinated multi-axis strike. Israel responded with strikes on Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon.

notes: Day 13. Coordinated multi-axis attack: rockets/missiles/drones from Lebanon (north) and Iran (east) simultaneously between 11:55-12:35 UTC. Israel struck Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon in response. 5 hours of sustained fire, 50+ targets across Israel. Iran targeted Palmachim Airbase, Ovda Airbase, Shin Bet HQ. CENTCOM: ~6,000 targets struck in Iran total by this date.

defence_context.notes: Day 12. Continued daily strikes on UAE. 3 cargo ships struck off Iran's coast same day.

notes: Day 12. Continued daily strikes on UAE. 3 cargo ships struck off Iran's coast including one in Strait of Hormuz. 21 confirmed Iranian attacks on merchant shipping by this date.

defence_context.notes: Day 12. IRGC claimed it fired 4 missiles at CENTCOM HQ including 2 targeting Camp Arifjan. Part of 37th wave of attacks.

notes: Day 12. IRGC claimed firing 4 missiles at US CENTCOM headquarters including 2 targeting Camp Arifjan in Kuwait. Described as Iran's 37th wave of attacks since Feb 28.

defence_context.notes: ADNOC forced to shut Ruwais refinery (922,000 bpd capacity) after drone strike set fire to industrial complex. Largest single energy infrastructure hit of the war so far.

notes: Day 11. Iranian drone strike hit Ruwais Industrial Complex in Abu Dhabi, housing UAE's largest oil refinery. ADNOC halted operations. Fire visible on satellite imagery. Major escalation in energy infrastructure targeting. Same day: Iran began laying mines in Strait of Hormuz. US destroyed 16 Iranian minelayers. Strait effectively closed since Mar 4 — tanker traffic down ~70%.

defence_context.notes: Drone strike on Al Seef residential building killed 29-year-old woman, injured 8. Separate strike near Sitra refinery injured 32 Bahrainis (4 serious).

notes: Day 11. Drone strike on residential building in Al Seef, Manama killed a 29-year-old woman and injured 8 others. Separate attack near petroleum refinery in Sitra injured 32 Bahraini citizens.

waves.notes: 10 ballistic missiles with multiple warheads targeting Tel Aviv. 124 people injured.

defence_context.notes: 10 ballistic missiles with multiple warheads fired at Tel Aviv. 124 people injured. Multiple warhead technology complicates interception.

notes: Day 11. Iran fired 10 ballistic missiles with multiple warheads at Tel Aviv. Israel reported 124 people injured. Multiple warhead missiles represent escalation in lethality.

defence_context.notes: Fire at Fujairah oil facility from interception debris. Satellite imagery confirmed storage tank damage.

notes: Day 10. Fire at Fujairah oil facility from interception debris. Satellite imagery (AA) shows storage tanks damaged at Fujairah Oil Industry Zone.

defence_context.notes: 15 drones intercepted including attempted attack on Riyadh diplomatic quarter. Shaybah drone intercepted, no damage.

notes: Day 10. Shaybah drone intercepted. Al-Kharj residential facility struck — 2 Saudi civilians killed (first Saudi civilian deaths). 15 drones intercepted over Riyadh/Al-Kharj area. Qatar condemned Al-Kharj strike.

defence_context.notes: Iran used cluster warhead missiles targeting Israel. IDF launched extensive airstrikes in three areas of Iran in response. Cumulative: 13 Israelis killed, 1,929 wounded.

notes: Day 10. Missile launch at Israel after Mojtaba Khamenei named new Supreme Leader. Iran used cluster sub-munition warheads. IDF responded with extensive airstrikes in 3 areas of Iran.

defence_context.notes: Drone struck Kuwait City high-rise. Fires at Kuwait International Airport and social security office. 7th US service member died from injuries sustained in earlier attack — died at Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia.

notes: Kuwait Mar 8. Kuwait City high-rise hit by drone. Fires at Kuwait International Airport and social security office. 7th US service member died from injuries at Prince Sultan AB Saudi Arabia.

defence_context.notes: Al Dhafra satcom facilities targeted again. Cumulative UAE totals as of Mar 8: 174 BMs (161 intercepted, 13 fell into sea), 689+ drones (645 intercepted, 44 impacted), 8 cruise missiles (all intercepted). Casualties: 4 killed, 112 injured.

notes: UAE Mar 8 continued strikes. Al Dhafra satcom facilities targeted again. Cumulative UAE totals now: 174 BMs, 689 drones, 8 cruise missiles.

defence_context.notes: First NATO interception of the conflict. Missile directed toward Turkey intercepted by NATO air defense systems.

notes: FIRST NATO INTERCEPTION. Iranian missile directed toward Turkey intercepted by NATO air defense. Marks potential escalation involving NATO member state.

waves.notes: 2 BMs toward Prince Sultan Air Base
6 drones toward Shaybah oilfield
1 drone east of Riyadh

defence_context.notes: All 9 projectiles intercepted/destroyed. Saudi air defences performed well. Significant reduction in attack volume vs Feb 28 — CENTCOM reported 83% decrease in drone attacks since day one.

notes: Saudi Arabia Mar 7. 2 BMs at Prince Sultan Air Base (Al-Kharj), 6 drones at Shaybah oilfield, 1 drone east of Riyadh. All intercepted. Volume significantly reduced — 83% drop per CENTCOM.

defence_context.notes: 9 of 10 intercepted. 1 reached ground in remote area.

notes: Qatar Mar 7. 10 drones from Iran, 9 intercepted. 1 reached ground in remote area — first Iranian drone to land in Qatar.

defence_context.notes: IRGC Navy sea-launched drone attack. Reportedly hit US satellite communication center and radars at Al Dhafra. US using Lucas drone (reverse-engineered Shahed-136) in combat for first time.

notes: UAE Mar 7. IRGC Navy drone attack on Al Dhafra Air Base — hit US satcom center and radars. US deployed Lucas drone (reverse-engineered Shahed, $35k unit cost) in combat for first time.

defence_context.notes: Single missile intercepted above Aqaba.

notes: Jordan Mar 7. Single BM intercepted above Aqaba. Minimal attack — Iran's capability severely degraded by this point.

defence_context.notes: Day 7. Continued daily strikes on UAE. Intensity declining from peak of first 3 days but attacks sustained.

notes: Day 7. Continued daily drone/missile attacks. Attack intensity declining from initial barrage but sustained at significant levels.

defence_context.notes: Day 7. Missiles at Tel Aviv and Haifa. Air-raid alerts triggered, no immediate casualties reported.

notes: Day 7. Iran launched missiles at Tel Aviv and Haifa, triggering air-raid alerts. No immediate casualties reported. Iranian ballistic missile capacity significantly degraded.

waves.notes: 131 drones from Iran mainland and IRGC Navy vessels
6 ballistic missiles

defence_context.notes: IRGC Navy launched drones from vessels — new attack vector. US destroyed Iranian 'drone carrier' ship same day.

notes: UAE Mar 5 wave. 131+ drones and 6+ BMs. IRGC Navy launched drone attacks from vessels — first sea-based OWA drone attack. US destroyed Iranian drone carrier ship in response.

defence_context.notes: Iran shifted to energy targets. Both LNG facilities hit — production halted.

notes: Qatar Mar 5. 14 BMs and 4 drones targeting ENERGY infrastructure. Mesaieed and Ras Laffan LNG facilities hit, production halted. Shift from military to energy targeting.

defence_context.notes: Iran escalated from military to energy targets in Bahrain. Main oil refinery hit.

notes: Bahrain Mar 5. Bapco Energies refinery on Sitra island hit by Iranian missile strike. Shift to energy targeting.

defence_context.notes: Continued Iranian missile attacks on Israel despite heavy degradation of launch capability. Multiple incoming missile attacks reported.

notes: Israel Mar 5. Continued missile and drone attacks despite 90% reduction in BM capability per CENTCOM. Tel Aviv and Nevatim Air Base targeted.

defence_context.notes: Day 5. Continued daily strikes. Iranian Islamic Resistance in Iraq claimed 29 operations with dozens of missiles/drones targeting enemy bases.

notes: Day 5. Continued strikes. Islamic Resistance in Iraq claimed 29 operations with 'dozens' of missiles and drones targeting 'enemy' bases. Strait of Hormuz effectively closed — tanker traffic collapsing.

defence_context.notes: Day 5. Hundreds of drones targeting Kuwait, Iraq, Saudi, UAE simultaneously.

notes: Day 5. Part of massive multi-country wave — hundreds of drones targeting Kuwait, Iraq, Saudi Arabia, UAE simultaneously. Oil fields targeted.

defence_context.notes: Day 5. Shelter-in-place for Jeddah, Riyadh, Dhahran. US Mission limited non-essential travel.

notes: Day 5. US Mission issued shelter-in-place for Jeddah, Riyadh, and Dhahran. Non-essential travel to military installations restricted. Part of mass multi-country drone wave.

defence_context.notes: Day 5. Islamic Resistance in Iraq operating alongside IRGC. Erbil International Airport and US Consulate targeted.

notes: Day 5. Drones/missiles targeted Erbil International Airport, US Consulate, and Baghdad coalition bases. Islamic Resistance in Iraq (Iranian-backed militias) actively participating alongside IRGC.

defence_context.notes: Day 5. Ballistic missile rate declining due to launcher destruction. Total 500+ ballistic/naval missiles + ~2,000 drones fired since Feb 28 (Fars News, Mar 5).

notes: Day 5. Declining ballistic missile rate as US/Israeli strikes destroy launchers. By Mar 5: 500+ ballistic/naval missiles and ~2,000 drones fired since Feb 28 (Fars News Agency).

defence_context.notes: Day 4. Continued daily barrage on UAE. Interception debris causing civilian damage in Abu Dhabi and Dubai.

notes: Day 4. Fourth consecutive day of strikes. Interception debris and falling projectiles damaging populated areas in Abu Dhabi and Dubai.

defence_context.notes: Day 4. 2 Iranian drones struck US Embassy in Riyadh — fire and minor damage. Saudi MoD confirmed attack.

notes: Day 4. Two Iranian drones struck the US Embassy in Riyadh, causing fire and minor damage. Saudi Defense Ministry confirmed. Continued strikes on Prince Sultan Air Base.

defence_context.notes: Day 4. Continued missile launches at Israel. Iranian missile capacity declining as US/Israeli strikes destroy launchers.

notes: Day 4. Continued missile strikes on Tel Aviv and central Israel. Analysts noting decline in ballistic missile launch rate — depletion of Iranian launcher stores under sustained US/Israeli bombing.

defence_context.notes: Saudi MOD did not release specific intercept figures. Both military and energy targets struck. Ras Tanura: debris from interception caused fire, refinery halted, oil prices spiked 7%.

notes: Saudi Arabia Mar 2. US Embassy Riyadh struck by 2 Iranian drones causing 'limited fire and minor material damages'. Ras Tanura refinery (world's largest oil processing) suffered fire from interception debris — refinery halted, oil prices spiked 7%. Saudi MOD withheld numbers.

defence_context.notes: Hezbollah joined the conflict from Mar 2. 192 attack waves toward Israel (rockets, missiles, UAVs, anti-tank missiles). 89 attack waves on Mar 6-7 alone. Coordinated with Iranian strikes.

notes: Hezbollah joins war from southern Lebanon, Mar 2 onward. 192 attack waves toward Israel including rockets, missiles, UAVs, and anti-tank missiles. 89 attack waves on Mar 6-7 alone. Coordinated with Iranian direct strikes.

defence_context.notes: Day 3. Continued heavy barrage. PJAK Kurdish forces launched offensive into Iranian territory.

notes: Day 3. Third consecutive day of heavy strikes on UAE. Satellite imagery from Mar 1-2 showed damage to Iran's Natanz Nuclear Facility. Kurdish PJAK forces launched military offensive into Iranian territory with thousands of troops.

defence_context.notes: Day 3. Continued attacks on US facilities and Kuwait airport.

notes: Day 3. Continued strikes on Camp Arifjan and Kuwait airport. Friendly fire incident: 3 US F-15s downed during chaotic air defense engagement.

defence_context.notes: Day 3. Drone strikes on Manama high-rises. 4 injured in Bahrain.

notes: Day 3. Drones struck high-rises in Manama. 4 people injured in Bahrain. Part of sustained daily attacks on all GCC states.

defence_context.notes: Day 3. QatarEnergy halted LNG production after drones hit Ras Laffan and Mesaieed. Missiles targeting Hamad Airport intercepted. Qatari F-15s shot down 2 Iranian Su-24 bombers.

notes: Day 3. QatarEnergy halted all LNG production after drone strikes at Ras Laffan and Mesaieed Industrial City. Missiles targeting Hamad Airport intercepted. Qatari F-15s downed 2 Iranian Su-24 bombers and intercepted multiple drone/missile strikes.

defence_context.notes: Day 3. Missiles struck Tel Aviv and Haifa as part of Operation True Promise IV.

notes: Day 3. Iran struck Tel Aviv and Haifa. Part of Operation True Promise IV — Iranian retaliation for killing of Supreme Leader Khamenei.

waves.notes: Day 1 (Feb 28): ~90 missiles. Khorramshahr-4 BMs (Mach 16) penetrated Israeli defenses. Fattah-2 hypersonic missile (Mach 5+, maneuverable) also used.
Day 2 (Mar 1): ~65 missiles. BM struck Beit Shemesh — destroyed synagogue and bomb shelter. 9 killed, 49 injured.
Day 3 (Mar 2): ~25 missiles. BM struck Tel Aviv residential block. 1 killed (home health aide), dozens wounded.
Day 4+ (Mar 3 onward): ~20 missiles per day. Diminishing volume but continued launches.

defence_context.notes: Air defence interceptors FAILED to shoot down Beit Shemesh missile — under investigation by IAF. ~500kg warhead. Deadliest Iranian strike on Israel in this conflict. IDF assessed Iran has ~2,500 BMs remaining.

notes: DEADLIEST DAY for Israel. Iran used Khorramshahr-4 BMs (Mach 16) that penetrated Israeli defenses and Fattah-2 hypersonic missile (Mach 5+, maneuverable). Beit Shemesh: BM destroyed synagogue and bomb shelter, 9 killed, 49 injured. Tel Aviv: BM hit residential block, 1 killed, dozens wounded. Air defence failed on Beit Shemesh intercept — under IAF investigation. Missile tempo: Day 1 ~90, Day 2 ~65, Day 3 ~25, Day 4+ ~20. Sirens across much of Israel.

defence_context.notes: Day 2. Continued barrage. Explosions heard in downtown Dubai. Abu Dhabi airport struck. 3 killed in UAE by this date.

notes: Day 2. Second day of Iranian barrage on UAE. Explosions heard in downtown Dubai. Strikes targeted Abu Dhabi airport and landmark sites. 3 killed, multiple injured. UAE air defenses engaging continuously.

defence_context.notes: Day 2. 2 people wounded by hostile drone hitting residential building. Airport and defense equipment attacked. 6 power lines knocked out.

notes: Day 2. Kuwait attacked — 2 wounded by drone strike on residential building. Kuwait International Airport and defense equipment targeted. 6 power lines went out of service.

defence_context.notes: Day 2. Drones struck Crowne Plaza Hotel in Manama. Attacks on US Navy base NSA Bahrain.

notes: Day 2. Iranian drones struck Crowne Plaza Hotel in Bahrain and targeted US Navy base NSA Bahrain. Part of multi-country barrage hitting all GCC states.

defence_context.notes: Day 2. Explosions heard in Doha. 16 people injured in Qatar by this date. Drones targeted Al Udeid Air Base.

notes: Day 2. Explosions heard in Doha for second day. 16 injured in Qatar. Drones targeted Al Udeid Air Base (largest US base in Middle East).

waves.notes: Initial drone salvo targeting Al Dhafra Air Base
Ballistic missile salvo — 165 BMs at UAE targets
Follow-up drone wave targeting Abu Dhabi and Dubai

defence_context.notes: No interceptor drones in Gulf theatre. Patriot and THAAD primary intercept. 35 drones penetrated defences — hit airports and hotels. At least 1 killed Abu Dhabi, 4 injured Dubai Palm Jumeirah.

notes: UAE initial wave. 165 BMs, 2 cruise missiles, 541 OWA drones. 35 drones got through. Civilian targets hit including airports and hotels. Al Dhafra Air Base (hosts US F-35s) primary target. AN/TPY-2 radar at Al Dhafra destroyed (~$500M asset). MQ-9 Reaper hangars damaged. Cumulative UAE totals as of Mar 8: 174 BMs (161 intercepted, 13 fell into sea), 689+ drones (645 intercepted, 44 impacted), 8 cruise missiles (all intercepted). Casualties cumulative: 4 killed, 112 injured. Cumulative UAE totals as of Mar 9: 1,422 drones launched (1,342 intercepted, 94.4%), 246 missiles (229 intercepted), 8 cruise missiles (all intercepted). Mar 8 alone: 17 BMs (16 intercepted), 117 drones (113 intercepted).

waves.notes: Ballistic missiles at Ali al-Salem — all intercepted
OWA drone swarm targeting Ali al-Salem and Kuwait International Airport

defence_context.notes: All BMs at Ali Al Salem intercepted. One drone hit Kuwait International Airport — minor injuries to employees, material damage to passenger terminal. 6 US Army reservists killed when drone struck a port facility. Camp Arifjan also targeted.

notes: Kuwait initial wave. 97 BMs and 283 OWA drones. Ali Al Salem Air Base (hosts US forces), Camp Arifjan, and Kuwait International Airport targeted. All BMs intercepted. Airport drone strike caused minor injuries. 6 US Army reservists killed when drone struck port facility. 11 killed total in Kuwait. Cumulative Kuwait totals as of Mar 9: 406 drones and 219 missiles targeted. 11 killed including 6 US Army reservists.

waves.notes: Ballistic missiles at 5th Fleet HQ — partially hit
Shahed drone wave targeting naval base and Manama

defence_context.notes: Bahrain called it 'blatant violation of sovereignty'. 5th Fleet HQ partially hit. Video showed Shahed striking tower near naval HQ. Multiple residential buildings hit.

notes: Bahrain initial wave. 61 BMs and 34 drones. US Navy 5th Fleet HQ directly targeted and partially hit. Residential buildings also struck. Cumulative Bahrain totals: 164 drones and 95 missiles intercepted. 3 wounded by missile debris over university in Muharraq. Desalination plant damaged.

waves.notes: Ballistic missiles at Al Udeid and early warning radar
Follow-up drone wave

defence_context.notes: Qatar MOD: 'all missiles intercepted'. 100% interception rate. Patriot batteries at Al Udeid primary defence.

notes: Qatar initial wave. 65 BMs and 12 drones. Al Udeid (CENTCOM forward HQ) primary target. Long-range early warning radar also targeted. 100% interception.

waves.notes: 13 ballistic missiles targeting Muwaffaq al-Salti Air Base — all intercepted
49 drones targeting Azraq air base and THAAD radar — all intercepted

defence_context.notes: All 62 projectiles intercepted on initial wave. 100% interception rate. Muwaffaq al-Salti Air Base (Azraq) attacked multiple times — all intercepted. US THAAD radar system in Jordan struck and destroyed in subsequent attacks.

notes: Jordan initial wave. 13 BMs and 49 drones. All intercepted. Muwaffaq al-Salti Air Base (Azraq) primary target — attacked multiple times, all intercepted. US THAAD radar system in Jordan struck and destroyed in subsequent attacks.

defence_context.notes: 0% interception — both drones hit. Oman had no Shahed-class air defence. Surprising attack given Oman's traditional mediator neutrality.

notes: Oman. 2 drones struck Duqm port (US Navy logistics hub). 0% interception. Surprising given Oman's neutral mediator role.

waves.notes: Ballistic missile salvo at Tel Aviv and Haifa — intercepted by Arrow and David's Sling
Drone wave across Iraq/Syria airspace toward Israel

defence_context.notes: Multi-layer defence: Arrow 3 (exo-atmospheric BM intercept), David's Sling (mid-range), Iron Dome (short-range/drones). IAF intercepted 100+ drones. NATO assets in Eastern Mediterranean also engaged — neutralised BM that entered Turkish airspace.

notes: Israel initial wave. Missiles and drones launched at Tel Aviv, Haifa, Nevatim Air Base, Ben Gurion Airport. Most intercepted by Arrow/David's Sling/Iron Dome. Explosions lit up Tel Aviv sky. IAF announced 100+ drone intercepts. NATO assets also engaged.

defence_context.notes: Erbil airport targeted twice. Hosts US and coalition forces. One drone intercepted.

notes: Iraq. Erbil International Airport targeted twice by drones. Hosts US and coalition forces.
